back_translation: Paul and Timothy, slaves of Lord Jesus Christ, to all the holy people in Lord Jesus Christ who are in the city of Philippi, together with the overseers and the servants
May grace and peace from God our Father, and from Lord Jesus Christ, abide with you
I give thanks to my God every time I remember you
In every prayer of mine for all of you, I have always prayed with joy
Because of your participation in the gospel, from the first day until now
I am confident of this, that He who began the good in you will accomplish it until the day of Lord Jesus Christ

It is fitting that I should think about all of you in this way, because I have you in my heart, both in the time when I am imprisoned and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel; all of you share in grace with me
God is my witness that I yearn for all of you with the loving heart of Lord Jesus Christ
And this is my prayer — that your love may abound more and more in true knowledge and in every kind of subtle understanding
So that you may test and choose what is excellent, and so that you may be people pure of heart and without blemish until the day of Lord Christ
Filled to overflowing with the fruit of righteousness, which comes through Lord Jesus Christ, for the glory and praise of God
Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that what has happened to me has actually become a cause for the gospel to spread out further
The result is that throughout the whole praetorian guard and to all the rest, it has become known that I am imprisoned because of Lord Christ
And most of the brothers and sisters in the Lord have received increased confidence because of my imprisonment, so they dare to proclaim the word without fear more than before
Some indeed proclaim Christ out of envy and rivalry, but others proclaim out of good intention
One side does it out of love, knowing that I am set in place for the defense of the gospel
But the other side proclaims Christ out of selfishness, not from a pure heart, thinking that they will cause additional distress to me while I am imprisoned
Then how shall it be? Only that — whether by any way, whether by pretext or by truth — Christ is proclaimed, and in this matter I rejoice; and I will continue to rejoice further
Because I know that this thing will turn into the rescue of me, through the prayer of you and the bestowal of the Spirit of Lord Jesus Christ

According to the focused-attentive watching and the hope of mine, that I will not have to be ashamed in anything at all, but with all boldness, Lord Christ will be exalted in the body of mine — just as it has always been, and now likewise — whether by living or by death
Because for me, to be alive is Christ, and to die is profit
But if to be alive in this body would mean fruitful work for me — then which shall I choose? I do not know
I am caught between these two paths; my heart leans toward departing from this world to be with Christ, which is a far better way
But for me to remain in this body further is more necessary for the sake of you
And being confident of this matter, I therefore know that I will continue on, and will remain together with all of you, for your progress and joy in faith
So that, by my returning to you again, your pride in Lord Jesus Christ concerning me will increase

Only let you live worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that whether I come and see you or am not present, I will hear about you that you stand firm in one spirit, with one heart, fighting together for the faith of the gospel
And not startled at anything coming from those who oppose you — this is a sign of their destruction, but of your salvation, and all this comes from God
Because you have received grace for the sake of Christ — not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for Him
Since you are fighting in the same battle as the one you formerly saw in me, and now hear that I am still fighting
